title: Docker: Writing Your First Dockerfile for a Python API
content:
### Day 182: Docker: Writing Your First Dockerfile for a Python API

**Dockerfile:** Recipe for building a Docker image.
```dockerfile
FROM python:3.10-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt
COPY . .
CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0"]
```
codeExample:
# Day 182: Docker: Writing Your First Dockerfile for a Python API
# Dockerfile for Python ML API
dockerfile = """
FROM python:3.10-slim

WORKDIR /app

# Install dependencies first (cache layer)
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Copy application code
COPY . .

# Expose port
EXPOSE 8000

# Run the API
CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
"""
print(dockerfile)

# Build and run commands
print("Commands:")
print("  docker build -t ml-api .")
print("  docker run -p 8000:8000 ml-api")
print("  docker ps  # list running containers")
keyTakeaways: Dockerfile: Recipe for building a Docker image.
